creator: gja@ansley.com
text:
Bruce,

Thank you for the insightful (and quick) analysis.  Obviously you have 
looked at this problem before. I agree that your suggested change would 
solve the problem with a minimal excess buffer size at higher speeds.  
It has been years (> 7) since I have poked around in sio.c so please 
forgive my naive analysis of the problem.

It is somewhat disturbing to discover that the potential interrupt 
latency is so large. My current project has processing deadlines of 
16ms, which with just serial and network traffic it has no problem meeting.

Because of other  requirements I am forced to run with hz=1000 with a 
relatively slow 400 Mhz CPU (I can't believe I calling a 400 Mhz CPU 
slow but I guess I showing my age :-( ) but you are correct that at 
hz=100 the buffers are sized so that there is not problem (Baud rate at 
125000).

On Monday I will test your suggested change in  the systems exhibiting 
the problem, but given that it provides for an even larger buffer sizes 
than my patch provided I sure it works.  Can you commit your change?  If 
not, what else needs to be done?

I also have a small patch that allows for non-standard baud rate crystal 
frequencies via "device hints" (I work on embedded systems that have 
many strange comm requirements).  Do you think that it would be possible 
to get that committed as well?

Greg
